body:
### Problem

the completion popupmenu, is cut off on the left side when cursor in first or second column
and completion item is too long to fit on screen


this also causes the following error:
```
> nvim file
free(): invalid next size (normal)
Aborted                    (core dumped) nvim file
~ (134)>
```

### Steps to reproduce

- put the following in your ~/.config/nvim/init.lua:
  ```
  vim.o.pumborder = "rounded"
  vim.o.autocomplete = true
  vim.o.completeopt = 'menuone,popup,noselect,fuzzy'
  vim.o.complete = '.'
  ```
- `nvim file`
- `iabc<esc>200Ax<esc>o` make sure it's longer than your window
- `a` triggers pum, which is cut off at the left side
  in this case it only cuts off the border (because the cursor is in the second column?)
  for whatever reason it also only cuts off the border if i <c-n> trigger it in the first column, but i managed in the past to trigger it with an lsp completion in the first column and it then also ate the first char of the completion item, but not when the cursor was in the second column (then only the border) and in the third column nothing
- quit and see the error and that it exited with code 134

### Expected behavior

pum starts on screen, not offscreen

no core dump

### Nvim version (nvim -v)

NVIM v0.12.1 Build type: RelWithDebInfo LuaJIT 2.1.1774896198 Run "nvim -V1 -v" for more info; the 0.12.0 appimage has the same issue

### Vim (not Nvim) behaves the same?

idk

### Operating system/version

archlinux

### Terminal name/version

foot version: 1.26.1 +pgo +ime +graphemes +toplevel-tag +blur -assertions

### $TERM environment variable

tmux-256color

### Installation

archlinux "extra" package, appimage downloaded from github

Alternative repro:

```vim
func Foo(f, b)
    return a:f ? 0 : ['a'->repeat(&columns-2)]
endfunc
set pumborder=single completeopt=menuone completefunc=Foo
call feedkeys("i\<C-X>\<C-U>", 'nt')
```
No truncation or crash in Vim.

labels.description: issue is fixed in vim and patch needs to be ported
labels.name: has:vim-patch

title: pum with border cut off and core dumped
